title: Expanding Features in Tidy Up
excerpt: It would be incredibly helpful to have an automation rule in the Tidy Up section under Administration that can review all boards across the account and take action based on certain criteria—specifically, the board’s age.…

posts.author: mayg (May Gresserman)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be incredibly helpful to have an automation rule in the Tidy Up section under Administration that can review all boards across the account and take action based on certain criteria—specifically, the board’s age. For example, the rule could identify boards that are older than 12 months and automatically archive them.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is a newer feature that gets pretty close to what you’re asking for — it’s called Scheduled Cleaning (Enterprise only).

It lives under:
Administration → Tidy Up → Scheduled cleaning

Instead of manual cleanup, you can now set an archival policy that automatically archives boards based on inactivity (e.g., “archive if no edits in X months”) — with an optional grace-period notification.

Here’s what it looks like:

image1895×980 146 KB

image855×574 33.1 KB

It’s still not truly date-based (you can’t say “archive anything created before Nov 2024”), but it does give admins automated lifecycle cleanup for old/inactive boards — a big step forward from Tidy Up being manual only.
